{{nihongo|'''Michinoku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|道奥菊多国造|''michinoku no kikuta no kuni no miyatsuko''}} was a [[kuni no miyatsuko]] who ruled '''Kikuta Province'''.

The kuni no miyatsuko's territory is thought to have been the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, centered on the southern part of present-day [[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] in [[Fukushima Prefecture]], and later became part of {{ill|Taka Province|ja|多珂国}} (which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|lt=Sukegawa}} in [[Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi]] to {{ill|Kumagawa|ja|熊川|lt=Kumagawa}} in [[Ōkuma, Fukushima|Ōkuma]]). When the [[ritsuryō]] system was established, the area was initially incorporated into [[Hitachi Province]], with its northern limit at "Kuma" (i.e. Kumagawa),<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima-ken no Chimei'', p. 205.</ref> and was later transferred to [[Mutsu Province]]. Owing to this history, until the beginning of the {{ill|Yamato court's northward expansion|ja|大和朝廷の北進}}, this land served as the northernmost reach of the [[Yamato Kingship]], confronting the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, and Kikuta Barrier, the predecessor of the {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|lt=Nakoso no Seki}}, is said to have stood there.

== Ancestry ==
According to Volume 10, the "Kokuzō Hongi", of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, during the reign of [[Emperor Ōjin]], '''Yanushi-Tone''', son of {{ill|Takekoro-no-Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed kuni no miyatsuko, marking the beginning of the line.

== Lineage ==
The '''{{ill|Hasetsukabe clan|ja|丈部氏}}''' (''hasetsukabe-uji''; [[kabane]] unknown); in {{ill|Jōgan (Japanese era)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 12 ([[870]]), 21 men and women of Kikuta District, including Hasetsukabe no Tsugimaro and Hasetsukabe no Hamanari, were granted the kabane '''Yue-Kikuta no [[Omi (rank)|Omi]]'''. The clan also included Yue-Hiri no Muraji and the Sōga-be clan.

== Tutelary shrine ==
* {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社 (いわき市)|lt=Kunitama Shrine}} – enshrined at 72 Baba, Nakoso-machi Kubota, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture (formerly Kubota-gō, Kikuta District), said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko.

== Notes ==
<references />

== See also ==
* {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
** ({{ill|Iwaki Province|ja|石城国}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|ja|浜通り}}) '''Kikuta no Seki''' ({{ill|Nakoso no Seki|ja|勿来関}}): Kikuta (Kikuta District) — {{ill|Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|lt=Iwaki}} ({{ill|Iwaki District|ja|磐城郡}}) — {{ill|Shineha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|lt=Shineha}} ({{ill|Shineha District|ja|標葉郡}}) — {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|lt=Ukita}} ({{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|lt=Uda District}}) — {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|lt=Iku}} ({{ill|Igu District|ja|伊具郡}}) — {{ill|Omoi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|lt=Omoi}} ({{ill|Shida District, Miyagi|ja|志田郡|lt=Shida District}})
** ({{ill|Iwase Province|ja|石背国}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|ja|中通り}}) '''Shirakawa no Seki''': {{ill|Shirakawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|lt=Shirakawa}} ({{ill|Shirakawa District|ja|白河郡}}) — {{ill|Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|lt=Iwase}} ({{ill|Iwase District|ja|岩瀬郡}}) — {{ill|Asaka no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|lt=Asaka}} ({{ill|Asaka District|ja|安積郡}}) — {{ill|Shinobu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|lt=Shinobu}} ({{ill|Shinobu District|ja|信夫郡}})
**: ※ From south to north. There is no kuni no miyatsuko named after {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} of Iwase Province or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|lt=Namekata District}} of Iwaki Province.
* {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}
* {{ill|Taka Province|ja|多珂国}}
* {{ill|Michinokuchi no Kihe no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}}
* {{ill|Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:陸奥国]]
[[Category:いわき市の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:道奥菊多国造]]
